questions.question_text: A property owner hires a friend to find a buyer for their home and promises to pay $5,000 upon sale. The friend does NOT hold a real estate license. This arrangement is:
distractors: Legal if the friend is a MA resident
Allowed if payment is after closing
questions.explanation: Any person who, for another and for compensation, negotiates or offers to negotiate the sale of real property must hold a license. The friend is engaging in licensed activity without a license.

questions.question_text: Which of the following persons is EXEMPT from the Massachusetts real estate licensing requirement?
questions.correct_answer: An attorney acting within their practice
distractors: A friend selling property for a fee
questions.explanation: Attorneys acting within the scope of their legal practice are exempt from licensing requirements. Friends receiving compensation, consultants, and property managers performing licensable activities need licenses.

questions.question_text: Which activity requires a Massachusetts real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Listing property for sale for compensation
distractors: Selling your own home without a broker
Appraising property as a licensed appraiser
questions.explanation: Listing, selling, purchasing, exchanging, or leasing real property for another and for compensation requires a license. Owners managing or selling their own property are exempt.

questions.question_text: A person advertises online that they can help renters find apartments for a fee. They hold no real estate license. This person is:
distractors: Exempt under the internet commerce rule
Legal if they disclose no license
questions.explanation: Advertising or holding oneself out as performing real estate services for compensation is a licensable activity. There is no internet commerce exemption from licensing requirements.

questions.question_text: An owner of a four-unit apartment building personally shows units and negotiates leases with tenants. Does this activity require a license?
questions.correct_answer: No, owners may manage their own property
distractors: Yes, all rental negotiations need a license
Only if the owner charges a rental fee
questions.explanation: Owners managing their own property are exempt from the licensing requirement. The license requirement applies to those performing real estate activities for another person and for compensation.

questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must a Massachusetts salesperson candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Massachusetts requires 40 hours of pre-licensure salesperson education from a Board-approved school. This is lower than many states that require 60-90 hours.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate needs three endorsements before taking the exam. Which person may NOT serve as an endorser?
questions.correct_answer: A classmate from the pre-license course
distractors: A neighbor of at least five years
A former employer from any job
An unrelated family friend or colleague
questions.explanation: Endorsers must be unrelated individuals, and classmates from the real estate course specifically may NOT sign endorsements. Neighbors, employers, and unrelated friends may serve as endorsers.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate passes the national portion but fails the state portion. What must the candidate do?
questions.correct_answer: Retake only the failed state portion
distractors: Retake both portions of the exam
Wait at least 6 months before retaking
questions.explanation: Massachusetts uses a split exam. A candidate who passes one portion but fails the other only needs to retake the failed portion, as long as they complete both within 2 years of their educational certificate.

questions.question_text: A candidate completes their 40-hour education on March 1, 2026. By what date must they pass the licensing exam?
questions.explanation: Candidates must pass both portions of the exam within 2 years of completing the educational certificate. March 2026 plus 2 years equals March 2028.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to apply for a Massachusetts salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Applicants for both salesperson and broker licenses must be at least 18 years old. This is consistent with most other states.

questions.question_text: A salesperson has been licensed for two years and wants to become a broker. Which requirement has she NOT yet met?
questions.explanation: Broker applicants must have been licensed and affiliated with a MA broker for 3 years working a minimum of 25 hours per week. Two years falls short of this requirement.

questions.question_text: A Massachusetts broker applicant must obtain what type of bond before receiving a license?
questions.explanation: Broker applicants must obtain a $5,000 surety bond (original form, no copies). This provides financial protection for consumers who may be harmed by the broker's actions.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum weekly work requirement for a salesperson to qualify their experience toward a broker license?
questions.explanation: Broker applicants must demonstrate experience working minimum 25 hours per week as a salesperson for at least 3 years under a MA broker.

questions.question_text: A broker applicant's salesperson experience ended 30 months ago. Can they still use this experience to qualify?
questions.correct_answer: No, experience cannot be more than 2 years old
distractors: Yes, there is no expiration on experience
Yes, as long as it is within 3 years
Only if the Board grants a special waiver
questions.explanation: The broker applicant's salesperson experience must be no more than 2 years old when the examination is taken. 30 months (2.5 years) exceeds this limit.

questions.question_text: A broker applicant submits a photocopy of their surety bond with their application. The Board will MOST likely:
questions.correct_answer: Reject it — an original bond is required
distractors: Accept it with a notarized cover letter
Accept it if certified by the surety company
Accept any legible copy of the bond form
questions.explanation: Massachusetts requires broker applicants to submit an original $5,000 surety bond — no copies are accepted. This ensures the bond is genuine and enforceable.

questions.question_text: A licensee fails to complete continuing education before their renewal date. What status will their license be renewed as?
questions.explanation: If CE is not completed before renewal, the license is renewed as inactive. An inactive licensee cannot practice real estate until CE requirements are met and the license is reactivated.

questions.question_text: Which license status means the holder exists in the system but cannot practice real estate?
questions.explanation: An inactive license exists but the holder cannot practice. An active license permits practice. An expired license means the term has lapsed entirely. Massachusetts does not use 'probationary' status.

questions.question_text: A salesperson lets their license expire. What must they do to practice real estate again?
distractors: Simply pay a late renewal fee
questions.explanation: An expired license means the license term has lapsed. Unlike an inactive license that can be reactivated, an expired license requires re-application, which may include retaking the exam.

questions.question_text: A Massachusetts real estate license expires on the licensee's date of birth and is valid for:
questions.correct_answer: No less than 2, no more than 3 years
distractors: No less than 1, no more than 2 years
questions.explanation: Massachusetts licenses expire on the licensee's birthday and are valid for no less than 2 years and no more than 3 years, depending on when the exam was passed relative to the birthday.

questions.question_text: A licensee holds an inactive license. Which statement is TRUE about their status?
questions.correct_answer: They may not perform any real estate activities
distractors: They may assist at open houses only
They may advertise but not negotiate
questions.explanation: An inactive licensee cannot practice real estate in any capacity. To resume practice, they must complete CE requirements and have their license reactivated to active status.
